comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report


Last Updated: 3/12/26 | 12:30PM

Good afternoon, Sunday River!

After a warm morning across the 8 peaks, temperatures are now dropping quickly. It looks like that little stretch of "fake spring" is behind us for now, and Mother Nature is sending winter back our way. If you're inside grabbing lunch, you may want to bring an extra layer for your afternoon turns.

After a few nights of a scaled-back grooming routine, the PistenBully fleet will be back out across the whole resort tonight putting stripes on close to 80 trails. Tomorrow morning's corduroy should be in great shape for first chair at 9 AM.

Looking ahead, the weekend is shaping up nicely. Saturday morning could bring a few fresh inches, setting us up for a solid stretch of skiing. And if the forecast holds, we may see another round of snow around midweek.

Last but certainly not least, it's time to renew your New England Pass for the 2026/27 season. Current passholders can lock in the renewal rate, and if you didn't have one this season, you can still grab one at the lowest price of the year. As an added bonus, buying now lets you ski the rest of this season, too. Either way, now is the time to get the most bang for your buck.

Winter is still very much alive. Come spend it with us.

-Team Snow

comment.name: Comment 2
comment.text:
Good morning, Sunday River!

It's a little wet out there to start the day, but don't let that scare you off. Things should clear up right around the time lifts start spinning at 9 AM, and there's even a chance we see a few snow showers drift through later this morning. Classic New England move.

Quick heads up on grooming. The crew has been dialing things back on their nightly routine lately, which means more ungroomed terrain out there for those who like a little texture under their skis. If you're the type who lives for that early morning corduroy, check out the trail status page to see exactly where the cats made their rounds last night before heading out.

Park riders, take note. The crew will be reworking Lower T72 during the day today with a cat. They're aiming to have everything dialed in ahead of the weekend, but that trail will be roped off for the time being while the work gets done.

As for temps, the mountain is doing its usual thing where every elevation seems to be living in its own season. Weather stations are currently showing 50 at Locke Peak, 53 at North Peak, 31 down at the Jordan base, and 34 at White Cap base. As the day goes on, things should level out a bit and cool down, with temps around 26 up high and about 32 at the lower elevations by mid-afternoon.

Looking ahead to the weekend, things are shaping up nicely. Forecasts are pointing toward snow moving in Friday night and sticking around into Saturday morning. A mid-March powder day in New England is the kind of thing you don't want to miss.

And before you go, if you want the important stuff like powder alerts and special offers sent straight to your phone, make sure you sign up for our new SMS text alerts so you're always in the loop.

We'll see you out there!

-Team Snow
